design.gap: GAP F — Authorization beyond the 3 presets (public/authenticated/owner + ownerField): claims-based role presets, org/team row scoping, field-level write rules, per-row sharing/ACL.
design.approach:
The mechanism is **parameterized presets, not expressions**. Today `collections.access` (db/schema.ts:92-96) holds string presets consumed by three pure gate functions (lib/access-rules.ts gateRead:27 / gateCreate:56 / gateMutate:85) that return either a denial or `{ok, user, ownerClause?}`, where ownerClause is a plain WhereClause prepended to effectiveWhere (app/api/v1/[collection]/route.ts:104-108) and mirrored JS-side for single reads ([id]/route.ts:55). Every extension below is a new *data shape* in the access JSONB plus a new branch in those gates — no new evaluation engine. The one parameterized shape is `ClaimRule = {claim: string, equals: string | string[]}`: matches when the verified JWT's top-level claim (string or string-array value, so Clerk `metadata.role`-style flat claims and role arrays both work) intersects `equals`. `verifyEndUser` already returns the full payload as `EndUser.claims` (lib/user-auth.ts:74), so zero auth-layer change is needed — verification stays purely cryptographic JWKS. Two hard rules govern every identity-derived value: (1) **fail closed at the gate** — if a configured rule needs a claim and the verified payload doesn't carry it as a usable (non-empty string, or string/string-array for ClaimRule) value, the gate returns 403 with a fix hint BEFORE any WhereClause is constructed, so undefined/empty/object values never reach buildWhere or its JS mirror matchesClauses; (2) **server-stamped identity fields are immutable through delivery** — the existing hard-coded ownerField strip in PATCH ([id]/route.ts:87-90) generalizes to a loop over all stamped identity fields (ownerField + org.field), and configurations where a stamped field could be populated anonymously are rejected at define time.

Ranking by real-world frequency: (1) **claim/role presets** — "only editors can write" is the first thing every member-content site needs beyond owner; (2) **org/team scoping** — the standard B2B shape, and Clerk tokens carry an org claim natively; (3) **field-level write rules** — moderation flags/status fields writable only by staff; (4) **per-row sharing/ACL** — genuinely rare for AgentX's tenant profile (branded sites, forms, member areas) and the only one that breaks the WhereItem vocabulary (needs EXISTS against a side-table). Recommendation: ship F1–F4, explicitly defer F5 with the side-table decision recorded.

Design of each rung. F1: `access.read`/`access.write` accept `ClaimRule` alongside the string presets; a claim-write grants create + PATCH/DELETE on ALL rows (role = staff, not owner-scoped) — ownerField becomes optional there but is still stamped for attribution when present. claimMatches fails closed: a claim that is absent, empty, or not a string/string-array is a non-match producing the same 403/E_SCOPE shape as a value mismatch, with the message distinguishing "JWT has no \"role\" claim" from "role=\"viewer\", expected \"editor\"". F2: `read`/`write` accept an **array of presets meaning any-of** (`write: ["owner", {claim:"role", equals:"moderator"}]`) — the ubiquitous "authors edit their own, moderators edit everything", mirroring the existing `anyOf` OR-group precedent in the where grammar; evaluation is "broadest passing preset wins, else owner fallback yields ownerClause". F3: `access.org = {claim, field}` — an orthogonal row-scope dimension composing with any read/write level. Its contract is uniform across ALL delivery operations: a new `requireOrgClaim(collection, user)` precondition runs in gateRead, gateCreate, AND gateMutate — if org is configured, the request must carry a verified user whose `claims[org.claim]` is a non-empty string, else 403 with the session-token-template hint; only then is the orgClause `{field: org.field, op:'eq', value}` emitted (Gate generalizes `ownerClause?` to `rowClauses: WhereClause[]`, three consumer sites). org.field is server-stamped on create (stampOwner generalizes to stampIdentity), stripped from PATCH bodies alongside ownerField, and org + anonymous-write configurations (write:'none' + publicWrite) are rejected at define time — so no path, create or update, authenticated or anonymous, can store a client-chosen org value. F4: per-field `writableBy?: "none" | ClaimRule` on FieldBase (lib/field-types.ts:21) — enforced ONLY on the delivery surface (admin/MCP stay trusted operator surfaces, exactly like publicFilter's "Admin/MCP reads unaffected" precedent), via a `checkFieldWrites(collection, user, payload)` helper called in delivery POST/PATCH before createEntry/updateEntry.

Visibility (the gap's hard requirement): describe_collection already returns `access` and `fields` verbatim (tools.ts:761,763), so every new shape is self-describing for free; each increment also updates define_collection's description + hand-written inputSchema (the known drift gotcha — one PR checklist item), and F1 adds an `accessPresets` block to get_project_info enumerating every supported preset shape with one-line semantics — including the fail-closed org rule and the stamped-field immutability — so an agent can discover the full rule vocabulary without reading docs. Runtime denials reuse the delivery envelope: 401 = no/invalid token, 403 = authenticated but rule not met (maps to E_SCOPE in CODE_BY_STATUS, delivery-http.ts:22 — its doc string gets broadened to "token or user lacks the required scope/role/claim"), with messages that name the claim, the expected value, the observed value when safe, and the Clerk-side fix.

keyDecisions.decision: One parameterized preset shape — ClaimRule {claim, equals: string|string[]} — added to the existing string enums; matching is flat top-level-claim equality/array-intersection only, and any claim value that is absent, empty, or not a string/string-array is a fail-closed non-match with the same 403/E_SCOPE shape as a mismatch (message distinguishes 'no such claim' from 'wrong value').
keyDecisions.rationale: Covers role-gating, role arrays, and multi-role any-of while staying fully validatable at define time and fully renderable in a tool description. EndUser.claims already carries the verified payload (lib/user-auth.ts:74), so no auth change. Uniform fail-closed matching means claim-absent and claim-mismatched produce one predictable denial shape an agent can branch on, and no coerced value ('[object Object]', 'undefined') ever participates in a comparison.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: Nested claim paths ('metadata.role') or predicate strings — dot-paths are the first step of an expression language; Clerk session-token templates can lift any nested value to a top-level claim, so the platform pushes that complexity to the IdP where it belongs. Also rejected: coercing non-string claim values via String() — that turns Clerk v2's nested 'o' object into a silent never-match or, worse, an accidental match.

keyDecisions.decision: Claim-based write grants mutation on ALL rows in the collection (subject to nothing but the claim and, when configured, the org gate), not owner-scoped.
keyDecisions.rationale: That is what 'role' means — editors edit everything. Owner-scoping a role would make it indistinguishable from write:'owner'. When access.org is configured it applies uniformly to every delivery identity including claim-write roles — one composition rule, stated in one sentence.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: Role-write restricted to own rows; the combined case ('own rows OR moderator') is exactly what F2's any-of preset array expresses explicitly. Also rejected: letting staff roles bypass org scoping — implicit exemptions can't be self-described and cross-org staff access is what MCP/admin surfaces are for.

keyDecisions.decision: Any-of composition is an ARRAY of presets, evaluated broadest-match-first, with 'owner' as the fallback that emits ownerClause.
keyDecisions.rationale: Mirrors the existing {anyOf:[clauses]} OR-group precedent in the where grammar (tools.ts:71); an array of enumerable presets is data, not syntax, and the evaluation rule fits in one sentence of tool description.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: Boolean rule trees ({allOf/anyOf/not}) — that IS an expression language; and merging role-write into gateMutate implicitly (magic 'or owner') — implicit semantics can't be self-described.

keyDecisions.decision: Org scoping is an orthogonal dimension (access.org = {claim, field}) with fail-closed claim presence enforced as a gate-time precondition on EVERY delivery operation (list, single read, create, update, delete): requireOrgClaim(collection, user) returns 403 with the claim-naming fix hint unless claims[org.claim] is a non-empty string, and only after it passes is any orgClause constructed. Gate generalizes ownerClause to rowClauses: WhereClause[].
keyDecisions.rationale: A missing org claim is the COMMON case (Clerk session tokens omit org claims whenever the user has no active organization), not an edge — and the value feeds two parallel where-implementations (buildWhere SQL vs matchesClauses JS) whose divergence on undefined/empty values would expose un-orged rows through the single-entry endpoint while the list excludes them. Checking presence once at the gate, before clause construction, guarantees both implementations only ever see a non-empty string — for op:'eq' they provably agree (SQL data->>'f' = $1 is false on missing keys; JS String(v ?? '') === nonEmpty is false on missing keys), so rows lacking the org field are invisible on both paths. A 403 with a hint also satisfies Rule 2 where a silent empty list would not.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: read:'org'/write:'org' preset values — cannot compose with owner or claim rules. Handling missing claims inside buildWhere/matchesClauses — splits one security check across two parallel implementations, the exact divergence gotcha this design flags. Treating a missing claim as 'matches nothing' (empty list / universal 404) — silent, hint-free, and violates Rule 2; the agent-repairable behavior is an explicit 403 naming the claim and the session-token-template fix.

keyDecisions.decision: Server-stamped identity fields (ownerField AND org.field) are immutable through the delivery surface: PATCH strips them via one loop over stampedIdentityFields(collection) — generalizing the existing hard-coded ownerField strip ([id]/route.ts:87-90) — and create always overwrites them via stampIdentity. Anonymous paths that could populate them are closed structurally: access.org combined with write:'none' + publicWrite is rejected at define time, and requireOrgClaim in gateCreate is the runtime backstop (no verified user ⇒ no claims ⇒ 403).
keyDecisions.rationale: The original F3 stamped org.field only on create, leaving two forgery paths: a row owner PATCHing {[org.field]: 'victim-org'} (org.field is an ordinary schema'd text field that passes validation) to inject rows into another org's views, and an anonymous publicWrite POST storing an attacker-chosen org verbatim (gateCreate returns user:null on that path, so stamping no-ops). Strip-on-PATCH plus define-time rejection plus the gate precondition closes both: after this, no delivery request — any method, any auth state — can persist a client-chosen value in a stamped field. Silent strip (not 403) matches the shipped ownerField precedent and is honest because the response body echoes the true stored values.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: 403 on PATCH bodies containing org.field (like F4's writableBy violations) — writableBy is a tenant-configured rule where a violation is a user error worth surfacing; stamped identity fields are platform-owned plumbing whose client-supplied values are meaningless, and 403ing them would break the shipped ownerField behavior clients already rely on. Also rejected: runtime-only 403 for the anonymous+org case without the define-time rejection — the misconfiguration would be discovered by end users hitting denials instead of by the defining agent at define time; the define-time E_VALIDATION with a fix hint ('set access.write to "authenticated" or "owner", or remove access.org') is the Rule-1/Rule-2-correct place, with the gate precondition kept as defense-in-depth.

keyDecisions.decision: Field-level write rules live on FieldDef (writableBy?: "none" | ClaimRule) and are enforced only at the delivery surface, before validation, via one helper in access-rules.ts. Stamped identity fields are exempt from checkFieldWrites (the server sets them; they are stripped, not rejected).
keyDecisions.rationale: Per-field knobs belong next to publicRead (the existing per-field precedent); admin/MCP are operator-trusted like publicFilter bypass; enforcing pre-validation keeps identity concerns out of buildEntrySchema so the one-definition-of-valid invariant is untouched. The exemption keeps the two mechanisms distinct: writableBy = tenant rule, violation is a 403 error; stamped field = platform-owned, client value ignored.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: Enforcement inside buildEntrySchema/valueSchemaFor — would need identity plumbed into the validation core and would break MCP/admin writes; and a separate access.fieldRules map — splits a field's contract across two JSONB blobs, hurting self-description in describe_collection.

keyDecisions.decision: Defer per-row sharing/ACL (F5); when built, use an entry_shares side-table, not a sharedWith field type.
keyDecisions.rationale: Lowest real-world frequency for AgentX tenants, and the only rung that breaks the machinery: 'owner OR shared-with-me' needs an EXISTS subquery the WhereItem vocabulary cannot express, plus new delivery endpoints and quota concerns. A side-table keeps entries.data purely schema'd, indexes cleanly, and revokes without rewriting rows.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: A sharedWith array field type — adds a 9th primitive with array semantics rippling through valueSchemaFor/OPS_BY_TYPE/admin Cell/EntryForm, makes shares part of validated user data (spoofable via any write path that doesn't strip it), and still needs the query-side EXISTS-equivalent.

keyDecisions.decision: Reuse 401/403 with the existing envelope: 403→E_SCOPE for authenticated-but-insufficient (doc string broadened), 401→E_AUTH for missing/invalid token; all fix hints in the message.
keyDecisions.rationale: CODE_BY_STATUS already maps these (delivery-http.ts:19-28); agents branch on 401-vs-403 (get a token vs get a claim), and E_SCOPE's meaning — 'lacks required scope' — genuinely covers role and org-claim denial. Codes are append-only; broadening a doc string is not a rename.
keyDecisions.alternativeRejected: New E_FORBIDDEN code — no new branching behavior for a client, just registry growth.

increments.name: Claim-based rule presets (read/write: {claim, equals})
increments.what: Extend access.read/access.write to accept ClaimRule = {claim: string, equals: string | string[]} alongside the existing strings. New helper claimMatches(user, rule) in lib/access-rules.ts: take user.claims[rule.claim]; if the value is not a string or array of strings, or is absent/empty, it is a fail-closed non-match (never coerce objects/numbers); otherwise normalize both sides to string arrays and match on non-empty intersection. gateRead/gateCreate: ClaimRule requires verified user + claimMatches, else denied(403) with a message that distinguishes absence from mismatch but keeps one shape: `requires claim "role"="editor" — the user's JWT has no "role" claim; add it via the Clerk session token template or user metadata` vs `...has role="viewer"...`. gateMutate: allow PATCH/DELETE when write is a matching ClaimRule (any row — role write is staff write); keep the owner path intact. Define-time: validateAccessAndEvents (lib/collections.ts:94) validates claim/equals non-empty and relaxes the ownerField requirement for claim-write (ownerField optional there; still stamped when present). Tool surface: defineArgs access zod (tools.ts:577-583) becomes a union with claimRuleSchema; the hand-written access inputSchema (tools.ts:140-155) gains the oneOf and description text; get_project_info gains an accessPresets block enumerating all preset shapes + semantics (including the fail-closed non-string rule), and userAuth text mentions claim rules. Verify: smoke test using startMockIssuer (scripts/smoke/helpers.mjs:110) minting JWTs (a) with {role:'editor'} → 201, (b) with {role:'viewer'} → 403 E_SCOPE naming observed value, (c) WITHOUT the claim → 403 E_SCOPE saying the claim is absent, (d) with a non-string claim value (object) → same 403 shape, against a write:{claim:'role',equals:'editor'} collection.
increments.surface: define_collection access schema + description; describe_collection returns new shape verbatim (no change needed); get_project_info new accessPresets block; delivery GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /v1/{collection}[/{id}] enforce it; 403 {error, code:'E_SCOPE'} with claim-name fix hint distinguishing absent vs mismatched.
increments.migration: none (access is JSONB; new shape is additive)

increments.name: Any-of preset composition (owner OR role)
increments.what: access.read/access.write additionally accept an ARRAY of presets meaning any-of, e.g. write: ["owner", {claim:"role", equals:"moderator"}]. Evaluation in the gates: normalize to a preset list; if any non-owner preset passes, full access (no ownerClause); else if "owner" is present and the user is authenticated, grant with ownerClause; else 403 listing every preset tried and why each failed (claim absent vs mismatched per F1 semantics). gateMutate mirrors: matching ClaimRule → any row; else owner preset → own rows only. Define-time validation: array non-empty, no duplicate presets, at most one "owner", ownerField required iff "owner" appears. Update defineArgs zod, JSON inputSchema, define_collection description, and the get_project_info accessPresets block. Verify: smoke — collection with write:["owner",{claim:'role',equals:'moderator'}]; author edits own row (200), author edits another's row (404), moderator edits anyone's row (200), token with neither ownership nor claim (403 listing both failures).
increments.surface: define_collection access schema/description; get_project_info accessPresets; same delivery endpoints — no new routes.

increments.name: Org/team row scoping (access.org) — fail-closed, tamper-proof
increments.what: New access.org?: {claim: string, field: string} — an orthogonal row-scope dimension applying uniformly to every delivery operation and every identity (including F1/F2 claim-write roles). Define-time (validateAccessAndEvents): org.field must name an existing text field (same pattern as the ownerField check, lib/collections.ts:105-116); read:'public' + org is rejected (public rows can't be org-scoped; hint: use read:'authenticated'); NEW: access.org combined with an anonymous write path (write:'none' — explicit or defaulted — while publicWrite is true) is rejected with E_VALIDATION and the hint 'org-scoped collections cannot accept anonymous writes: set access.write to "authenticated" or "owner", or remove access.org/publicWrite' — this closes the anonymous org-injection path structurally. Runtime, three pieces: (a) requireOrgClaim(collection, user) precondition in gateRead, gateCreate, AND gateMutate — if org is configured and (no verified user, or typeof user.claims[org.claim] !== 'string', or it is empty) return denied(403, `token has no "org_id" claim — the user needs an active organization, or add the claim to the Clerk session token template`) BEFORE any clause is constructed; a nested/object claim value (Clerk v2 'o') hits the same 403 with the same lift-to-flat-claim hint. This precondition also makes any residual anonymous create 403 as defense-in-depth behind the define-time rejection. (b) Gate type replaces ownerClause?: WhereClause with rowClauses: WhereClause[] — gateRead pushes orgClause {field: org.field, op:'eq', value: claims[org.claim]} (guaranteed non-empty string) for ALL reads plus ownerClause when owner applies; consumers updated: effectiveWhere composition (app/api/v1/[collection]/route.ts:104-108), single-read JS check ([id]/route.ts:55 becomes a loop over rowClauses via matchesClauses), and gateMutate adds entryData[org.field] === claims[org.claim] to its entry-data check (mismatch → 404, never 403). Because the clause value is always a non-empty string, the SQL path (data->>'f' = $1) and JS mirror (String(v ?? '') === value) agree that rows lacking the org field match neither — un-orged rows are invisible on both list and single GET. (c) Identity stamping + immutability: stampOwner generalizes to stampIdentity(collection, user, data) stamping both ownerField and org.field from verified identity on create (client values always overwritten — the gate precondition guarantees the claim exists); new stampedIdentityFields(collection) helper returns [ownerField, org.field].filter(present), and the delivery PATCH body strip ([id]/route.ts:87-90) becomes a loop over it, so org.field (like ownerField today) is silently stripped from PATCH bodies — a row owner can no longer inject their row into another org. MCP/admin remain trusted surfaces and may set org.field directly (documented in define_collection description). Tool surface: access inputSchema + description (stating uniform org application, fail-closed claim requirement, and PATCH-strip behavior), accessPresets block, userAuth text. Verify: smoke — (1) two mock-issuer users with different org_id claims: each lists only own-org rows; cross-org single GET is 404; (2) create with a spoofed body org value → stored value is the JWT claim; (3) PATCH body containing org.field → 200 but field unchanged (stripped), response echoes true value; (4) authenticated token WITHOUT the org claim → 403 E_SCOPE on list, single GET, create, PATCH, and DELETE, message naming the claim; (5) token with object-valued org claim → same 403s; (6) rows lacking the org field entirely are invisible on BOTH list and single GET for every token shape; (7) define_collection with access.org + publicWrite + write:'none' → E_VALIDATION with the fix hint; (8) define_collection with read:'public' + org → E_VALIDATION.
increments.surface: define_collection access.org (+ two new define-time E_VALIDATION rejections with fix hints); describe_collection verbatim; get_project_info accessPresets + userAuth text; delivery list/single/create/mutate all org-gated with fail-closed 403 E_SCOPE when the claim is missing/malformed; PATCH strips org.field like ownerField; no new endpoints.
increments.migration: none (JSONB; org values live in an ordinary text field on entries)

increments.name: Field-level write rules (writableBy on FieldDef)
increments.what: FieldBase gains writableBy?: "none" | ClaimRule (lib/field-types.ts:21-44; document in COMMON_FIELD_CONFIG so list_field_types serves it automatically). "none" = never writable via the delivery API (admin/MCP unaffected). ClaimRule = writable via delivery only when the verified user matches (F1 claimMatches semantics: absent/non-string claim = fail-closed non-match). fieldDefSchema (lib/validation.ts:38-93) validates the knob shape. New checkFieldWrites(collection, user, payload): returns the offending field names; called in delivery POST (app/api/v1/[collection]/route.ts, after gateCreate, before stampIdentity/createEntry) and PATCH ([id]/route.ts, after gateMutate and after the stamped-identity strip) — violation → 403 `fields [status] are writable only with claim "role"="moderator"; remove them from the payload or sign in with that role` (code E_SCOPE). Anonymous publicWrite submissions fail ClaimRule fields by definition (no user). Stamped identity fields (ownerField, org.field) are exempt — they are server-set and stripped, never checked (see F3). define_collection description gains one sentence; describe_collection exposes it via fields verbatim. Admin UI: one small chip next to VisibilityPill in components/EntryForm.tsx showing 'delivery: role-gated / read-only' — display only, no enforcement in admin. Verify: smoke — PATCH containing a writableBy:'none' field → 403 naming the field; same PATCH without it → 200; moderator JWT writes the ClaimRule field → 200; JWT without the claim → 403.
increments.surface: list_field_types (auto, via COMMON_FIELD_CONFIG); define_collection description; delivery POST/PATCH enforcement with per-field 403 hints; admin EntryForm visibility chip.

increments.name: Per-row sharing/ACL (entry_shares side-table) — RECOMMEND DEFER
increments.what: Deliberately not built now. Recorded shape for when demand appears: entry_shares table (id, projectId FK cascade, entryId FK cascade, subjectId text = JWT sub, role 'read'|'write', unique(entryId, subjectId)); delivery endpoints POST/DELETE /v1/{collection}/{id}/shares gated to the row owner; read gate extends the owner preset with an opt-in access.sharing:true so the owner clause becomes 'owner OR EXISTS(share)'. It is L because the EXISTS predicate cannot be expressed in the WhereItem vocabulary — it needs a new internal (non-user-facing) clause kind threaded through buildWhere AND its JS mirror matchesClauses in lockstep, plus share-count quotas and cursor-pagination interplay. If prioritized, split: (a) schema + owner-managed share endpoints, (b) read-gate EXISTS integration, (c) write-role shares. Until then, F1–F3 cover 'editors of this doc'-lite via org roles, which is what AgentX's actual tenants ask for.
increments.surface: (deferred) new /v1/{collection}/{id}/shares endpoints; access.sharing flag in define_collection.
increments.migration: entry_shares table via npm run db:push (when built)

design.invariantCompliance: Rule 1 (declarative + self-describing): every new rule is data in collections.access or FieldDef JSONB — describe_collection already returns both verbatim (tools.ts:761,763) so new shapes are visible with zero extra code; each increment updates define_collection's description AND hand-written inputSchema in the same PR (the known JSON/zod drift gotcha is a checklist item); F1 adds an accessPresets block to get_project_info enumerating every supported preset shape with one-line semantics — explicitly including the fail-closed rules ('org-gated operations 403 when the claim is missing or non-string') and the stamped-field immutability ('ownerField and org.field are server-set; delivery PATCH ignores them') so agents cannot infer weaker semantics than the platform enforces; the BOUNDARIES constant is amended to stop claiming 'no authorization rules'. Rule 2 (machine-readable errors with fix hints): define-time failures are ValidationError E_VALIDATION with the exact fix, including the two new contradiction rejections ('org-scoped collections cannot accept anonymous writes: set access.write...', 'read:\"public\" cannot combine with access.org...'); runtime denials use the existing {error, code} envelope — 401/E_AUTH = get a token, 403/E_SCOPE = get a claim — and every claim-related 403 names the claim, the expected value, whether the claim was absent, malformed (non-string), or mismatched (with the observed value when safe), and the Clerk-side remedy (session token template / user metadata); a missing org claim is an explicit 403 on every operation, never a silent empty list or an unexplained 404 divergence; org/owner row misses stay 404 to never confirm existence. Rule 3 (secrets are references): nothing new touches secrets — claim evaluation reads the already-verified JWT payload (lib/user-auth.ts:74); JWKS verification is unchanged and cryptographic-only; connectors and list_connectors are untouched. Rule 4 (destructive = plan + confirm): access changes are non-destructive metadata, applied immediately like publicFilter today; the destructive interaction — dropping/retyping a field referenced by ownerField, access.org.field, or a writableBy rule — is caught at define time by extending the existing ownerField-must-exist check in validateAccessAndEvents (lib/collections.ts:105-116), which runs before the diff/confirm gate, so a redefinition can never silently orphan a rule. Rule 5 (strict validation never weakens): gates run BEFORE validation and projection runs AFTER — buildEntrySchema, verifyRefs, and toPublicView are untouched; owner/org rowClauses are plain WhereClauses compiled by the existing parameterized buildWhere (no raw SQL; the JS mirror matchesClauses handles single reads identically), and the gate-level claim-presence precondition guarantees every clause value is a non-empty string, so neither implementation ever evaluates undefined/empty/coerced values and their eq semantics provably agree; stamped owner/org values pass through normal entry validation like any data; the PATCH-side stamped-field strip and checkFieldWrites only ever REMOVE write capability on the delivery surface — they cannot admit anything validation would reject, and MCP/admin writes still hit the same single definition of valid.
crossGapDependencies: None hard — no jobs runner, no streaming, no new infrastructure; everything is JWT-claim evaluation + WhereClause composition in the request path.

Coordination only: any delivery-API gap that reworks effectiveWhere composition or adds cursor pagination to GET /v1 (app/api/v1/[collection]/route.ts:104-118) must land against the F3 rowClauses shape (or vice versa) — same ten lines of code. Likewise anything touching the delivery PATCH body pipeline ([id]/route.ts:79-96) must preserve the stamped-identity-fields strip loop.

F1-F4 assume the shipped BYO-Clerk connector + verifyEndUser seam (auth-connectors subsystem) as-is; if a multi-IdP gap generalizes getAuthConfig, claim rules keep working unchanged because they only read the verified payload — and the fail-closed non-string matching means an IdP with differently-shaped claims degrades to explicit 403s, not silent misbehavior.
If an admin-UI gap adds a schema/rules viewer, the access JSONB + writableBy knobs from these increments are the data it should render — no joint work needed now beyond the F4 EntryForm chip.
risks: Clerk session tokens do NOT include custom claims by default — a tenant who sets write:{claim:'role',equals:'editor'} without editing the Clerk session token template locks out everyone. With fail-closed semantics this is now a consistent, hint-carrying 403 on every operation (never a silent empty list), but it is still a lockout; mitigation: the 403 message and get_project_info accessPresets both spell out the session-token-template step; consider a define-time warning in the tool result when a claim rule or access.org is set ('verify the claim appears in your Clerk session token template').

Claim-shape drift across IdP versions (Clerk v1 org_id string vs v2 nested 'o' object, roles as string vs array). The design fails closed — non-string values are explicit 403s naming the claim, string-or-string-array is matched — so an upgrade breaks loudly with a repair hint rather than silently; but 'loudly' still means downtime for that tenant's org-scoped reads until the template is fixed. The 403 message must name both the claim looked for and the shape found.

gateMutate today does not evaluate publicFilter, so a claim-write role can PATCH rows that are publicly invisible — pre-existing behavior that F1 widens from 'owners' to 'role holders'; acceptable (staff semantics) but must be stated in the define_collection description.

buildWhere / matchesClauses remain parallel implementations. F3's precondition removes the known divergence class (undefined/empty/non-string values) by construction, and eq-on-non-empty-string is the only org comparison, but the parallelism itself persists for future operators — smoke tests must still cover list AND single GET for every gate combination (the F3 verify list enumerates them, including no-claim and un-orged-row cases on both paths).

Silent PATCH-strip of org.field (matching the ownerField precedent) means an agent that sends the field gets a 200 without the field applied; the response body echoes the true stored values and describe_collection/accessPresets document the strip, but an agent that ignores response bodies could believe a write succeeded — the define_collection description must state 'ownerField and access.org.field are ignored in delivery PATCH bodies' verbatim.

F4 on PATCH can only block touching a field, not require keeping it (shallow-merge + partial validation semantics, lib/entries.ts:204) — a writableBy field can never be force-maintained by a role; document to avoid agents assuming otherwise.

Preset union growth makes the hand-written TOOL_DEFS inputSchema and the zod schemas easier to desynchronize (known gotcha); each increment's PR must update both plus the accessPresets block, or self-description silently lies.

Legacy data: enabling access.org on a collection with pre-existing rows leaves un-orged rows invisible to every delivery consumer (correct fail-closed behavior, but surprising) — the define_collection result for an org-enable on a non-empty collection should include an informational note with the count of entries lacking the org field (reusing the countEntriesWithKeys pattern, lib/collections.ts:218) and the hint to backfill via MCP.

Deferring F5 means 'share this doc with one user' has no answer beyond org membership; if a real tenant hits it early, resist the sharedWith-field shortcut — the side-table design is recorded in F5 precisely so the pressure doesn't produce a validated-data ACL.

openMinor.issue: F1's claimMatches ('normalize both sides to arrays of strings') is underspecified for non-string claim values. JWT claims can be numbers, booleans, or objects; naive String() coercion yields surprises like '[object Object]' or 'true', and a claim rule {claim:'email_verified', equals:'true'} silently matching a boolean is undocumented behavior an agent cannot predict from the tool description.
openMinor.suggestedFix: Define claimMatches to fail closed: only string values and arrays of strings participate in matching; numbers/booleans/objects/nested arrays never match, and the 403 hint says so ('claim "x" is not a string — lift it to a top-level string claim via the Clerk session token template'). State this one-sentence rule in the get_project_info accessPresets block.

openMinor.issue: F2's define-time validation ('array non-empty, no duplicates, at most one owner, ownerField iff owner') does not forbid dominated combinations: read:["public", {claim:...}] or write:["authenticated","owner"] make the narrower preset dead code under broadest-match-first evaluation, so describe_collection would self-describe a rule that never applies — quietly violating Rule 1's 'tool surface states boundaries out loud'.
openMinor.suggestedFix: Reject arrays containing 'public' (it dominates everything) and arrays where 'authenticated' appears alongside 'owner' or a ClaimRule for the same verb, with an E_VALIDATION fix hint naming the dominating preset ('"authenticated" already grants what "owner" would — remove one'). Alternatively document domination explicitly in accessPresets, but rejection is cheaper and unambiguous.

openMinor.issue: F1 widens the delivery mutation surface (claim-write grants PATCH/DELETE on ALL rows to any user holding the claim) but rate limiting only guards POST (app/api/v1/[collection]/route.ts:147); PATCH and DELETE in [id]/route.ts have no rateLimit call. A single compromised or malicious claim-holding end-user token can bulk-delete or bulk-mutate an entire collection at full speed, and each mutation fans out webhooks/emails (lib/events.ts) — an amplification vector the 20-req/60s window was meant to bound on the write path.
openMinor.suggestedFix: Add the same rateLimit(`${projectId}:${ip}`) check to PATCH and DELETE handlers in app/api/v1/[collection]/[id]/route.ts as part of F1 (the increment that first enables non-owner-scoped mutation), and note it in the increment's verify step.

openMinor.issue: F4 allows writableBy:'none' (or a ClaimRule) on a field that is also required: delivery creates then become impossible for non-matching users — checkFieldWrites 403s the field if present, and buildEntrySchema 422s if absent (required is create-enforced, lib/validation.ts). An agent gets two contradictory machine-readable errors with no hint pointing at the schema-level contradiction, violating the 'repair from the error alone' bar.
openMinor.suggestedFix: At define time (validateFieldDefs / fieldDefSchema superRefine), reject required:true combined with writableBy:'none', and emit a warning in the define_collection result when required combines with a ClaimRule writableBy ('only users with claim X can create entries in this collection'). One sentence in FIELD_TYPE_SPECS/COMMON_FIELD_CONFIG documents the interaction.

openMinor.issue: F2's array-member vocabulary is under-specified: the validation rules say 'non-empty, no duplicates, at most one owner' but never state whether the scalar presets 'public', 'authenticated', and 'none' are legal array members. read:['public', {claim…}] is a degenerate contradiction (public grants everything, making the claim rule dead weight), and write:['authenticated', 'owner'] has ambiguous gateMutate semantics ('authenticated' grants create-only today, lib/access-rules.ts:84-92 allows mutation only under write:'owner'). An implementer must invent these rules, and whatever they invent must be renderable in the tool description per Rule 1.
openMinor.suggestedFix: State the array grammar explicitly in F2: array members are restricted to 'owner' | ClaimRule (scalar 'public'/'authenticated'/'none' remain scalar-only), enforced in validateAccessAndEvents with a fix-hint ValidationError, and reflected in both the zod union and the hand-written inputSchema oneOf. One sentence in the design and one sentence in define_collection's description close it.

openMinor.issue: F4 creates an unvalidated contradiction: a field that is required (create-only, lib/validation.ts:204) AND writableBy:'none' (or a ClaimRule the submitting population can't hold, e.g. any ClaimRule on an anonymous publicWrite form) makes every delivery create impossible — include the field and get 403 from checkFieldWrites, omit it and get 422 required-missing from buildEntrySchema. Neither error tells the agent the schema itself is self-contradictory, so the fix hint (Rule 2) points at the wrong layer.
openMinor.suggestedFix: Add a define-time check in validateAccessAndEvents/fieldDefSchema: reject required:true + writableBy:'none' outright, and warn (or reject) required + ClaimRule on collections whose write path admits anonymous submissions (publicWrite with write:'none'). Message names the field and says 'make it optional, stamp it server-side, or drop writableBy'.

openMinor.issue: F1's claim-write widens a read/write asymmetry beyond the acknowledged publicFilter risk: gateMutate under a matching ClaimRule grants PATCH on any row, and the PATCH handler returns toPublicView of the updated entry ([id]/route.ts:97-98) — so a role-holder receives row data that the read gate (read:'owner' or a read ClaimRule they don't hold) would 404 on the very same row via GET. Risk item 3 covers the publicFilter bypass but not this read-gate bypass; the define_collection description sentence promised there should cover both, or agents will model read rules as authoritative for role-writers when they are not.
openMinor.suggestedFix: Broaden the promised define_collection description sentence to state both: 'a matching write ClaimRule grants mutation on all rows regardless of read rules and publicFilter; the mutation response reveals the row's public fields'. Alternatively (cleaner): have claim-authorized PATCH return {id} only, matching POST's 201 {id} shape, when the caller would fail gateRead for that row — but that costs an extra gate evaluation; the documentation route is the 20% solution.
